data.epi_content:
# Leaving the Crown Prince behind, they boarded the carriage once more.

Inside the rattling carriage, Evan looked at Vivian with slightly unfocused eyes, as if making a promise to himself.

"Help me out. You might not know the theory, but when it comes to casting spells and handling power, you're better than me, Vivian."

Vivian couldn't nod or even respond. After witnessing such a scene with her own eyes, she couldn't imagine what this seemingly unfazed person was about to ask. Evan was essentially an idiot who closed the stable door after the horse had bolted.

"What are you trying to do now...?"

"I don't know. I'll create some kind of small interference in the past somehow."

"How?"

"If I pour my entire life into it, I have to hope something will come of it."

"Why is everything suddenly changing like this? Look, Evan, I'm suffering. Can we talk about this later? Right in front of us, someone I thought was a friend—I guess I was the only one who thought that—shot herself in the head and died... ugh!"

Was it motion sickness or the swaying carriage? Vivian covered her mouth with her hand, trying to hold back the rising nausea. Some contents nearly spilled out, but she quickly conjured fire to burn it away, leaving nothing visible—a small consolation.

"Why. Why. Why do you keep talking like this? We need time to collect ourselves and mourn. We need time to pray for her soul and remember her. Show some respect for the dead!!"

Vivian became so emotional that she forgot her formal speech and didn't even realize she was shouting informally. In her mind, the Evan before her was alien, irrational, and nothing but a madman who had not even a speck of respect for the dead. Though Evan's impression of Vivian wasn't much different.

"What respect? Erica isn't dead. You saw it too. She just crossed over to somewhere different from us, that's all."

"But can we see her again here...?"

"......"

Evan couldn't answer confidently. He was still organizing his thoughts.

"Evan, then Erica is dead after all. At least she's dead here! What do you mean she's alive? By that logic, my father who passed away long ago must be living well in heaven or hell!"

Whether Erica had turned back time or regressed with her memories into a new body after death, Vivian considered her dead. At the very least, they would never meet her again in this world.

The difference between Evan and Vivian was that Evan chose to use even forbidden magic—though ironically, he despised black magic despite prying into the minds of the dead—to either send a message back in time or go back himself, even if resurrecting Erica was impossible.

Meanwhile, Vivian chose to accept that Erica was dead, would return to dust, and that the path forward was to bury the dead in her heart and move on. No matter how precious the deceased was, violating them by probing their mind like Evan suggested was an insult to the dead. People eventually die, and after accepting this fact, one should cherish their memory, endure the sadness when remembering them, and commemorate them by recalling the happy days spent together—this was the natural order of life.

"But, but you saw it too. You saw it too, Vivian. Erica is alive. With the memory of blowing her own head off. With that kind of terrible memory etched in her mind that she once mumbled to me about. She's living with the memory of dying horribly, miserably, very slowly. In fact, we've probably repeated the same thing with Erica many times. That's how it is! You saw it too!"

Their difference was that Vivian had already lost someone precious, buried them in her heart, and moved on. Evan, however, had spent over ten years with someone he considered family, and his mind was too twisted with guilt for having pushed her away despite their familiarity to think like Vivian.

"So, how is that different from those who look down on us from heaven..."

Vivian was about to ask what difference there was between the dead looking down from heaven or hell and Erica going back in time to relive her terrible experience.

But Evan, almost convulsing, like a convert denying his former beliefs, launched into a tirade, blinking like a paranoid person, waving his index finger, interrupting Vivian who used to listen to him so attentively.

"There's no such thing as heaven. It's unclear if God even exists. Well, there might be something transcendent. Whether it has free will is unknown. Seeing how Erica turned back time... But the world is all about human will. There is no omnipotent, singular God. Otherwise, how could I use healing magic, and how could there be necromancers, black magicians, and fanatics who believe in gods considered heretical?"

To Vivian, Evan had completely lost his mind. Denying God? Then what about the holy power that heals people and the paladins who emit brilliant light? Human will was ultimately that of God's creation, so it was God's will. Did that mean human will becomes God's will?
data.epi_content2:
Vivian was momentarily confused. She had never entertained such heretical thoughts before, having lived a life where God's existence was taken for granted.

"That's..."

If it had been someone else—someone who wasn't a magician—Vivian would have pointed at Evan and screamed that he was a black magician speaking falsehoods. But she was a magician who explored and recreated existing reality.

"See, you can't answer with certainty either. There's no such thing as certainty in this world."

To Evan, Vivian was insane. She called herself a friend but had essentially given up on a friend who might be trapped in time, declaring her dead.

They couldn't understand each other. If this had been a love story between a man and woman, they could have adjusted the parts they didn't understand, reconciled through conversation, and moved forward. They could have peacefully shared their opinions and facts.

But death was too intense for such peace. Especially when their mutual friend had shown everyone that she couldn't bear the weight of life's pain and took her own life in front of them all.

And intense, shocking things—whether events, words, or death—tend to bring out extremes.

"If He were truly omnipotent, He wouldn't have left Erica like that, wouldn't have placed a dull, crippled person like me beside her, and would have ensured everyone lived happily without suffering in pain. Until that black magician revealed what was inside the head, people thought the head just helped you look forward and speak. It was common sense that one thought with the heart. But one black magician discovered how the head works, and it was true. So this must be true too."

Evan uttered a paradox that would make paladins draw their swords against him instantly.

Is God willing to prevent evil but unable? Then He is not omnipotent.

Is He able but unwilling? Then He is malevolent.

Is He both able and willing? Then where does evil come from?

Is He neither able nor willing? Then why call Him God?

"Evan, you..."

"I know, if I said this in the middle of the street, I'd be stoned to death. Or they'd call me a necromancer, black magician, demon, and all sorts of nonsense while trying to kill me."

Evan said with a hardened expression.

"Is there a way for us to see Erica again? It's impossible unless she's resurrected, right?"

At this point, Vivian had already fallen for Evan's words.

This is why the church's paladins learn from childhood not to respond when dealing with pagans or those without religion. Mixing with them plants seeds of evil in what they call the mind's heart, leading to corruption.

So from a devout believer's perspective, the seed of corruption had bloomed in Evan's heart due to Erica's death, and it had infected Vivian as well.

"There is, so promise you'll help me later."

Caught up in the momentum, Vivian nodded hesitantly.

...

After returning to the Academy, Evan attended Erica's funeral once with Vivian, then only showed his face in class while spending all day locked in his room.

His room was so filled with books that there was barely space to step. Time continued to pass.

1 year, 2 years, 4 years, 8 years...
data.epi_content3:
He continued his research endlessly, neither accepting a suitable position from his family nor challenging for the head position, simply remaining at the Academy to continue his research. To facilitate his research further, he became a professor, gaining access to more books.

He heard that Vivian, the woman he once loved, had married a lowly person with half-Black blood, but he didn't care much. By then, only traces remained of his love, mission, or feelings for Erica—he was moving mechanically, fueled by the intense memory of witnessing death.

Going back in time or sending something to the past was more difficult than magic that could move stars capable of destroying entire cities.

After all, it went against the laws of the world. And those who best defied the world's laws were the corrupted magicians called black magicians.

Believing there was no answer through normal methods, Evan abandoned his professor title at the Academy and secluded himself in the mountains. Using his impressive magic, he created the cabin from a book that Erica had once said she wanted to live in.

Like black magicians often did, he continued experimenting on slaves, residents, and even lower nobles from the surrounding area. Finally, he discovered a way to send a tiny thought to the past, though he couldn't transfer his entire mind or experiences.

He completed preparations for the spell with some materials. Then he met Vivian, who was at the peak of her beauty, and familiar-looking knights. Beside Vivian stood the knight of slave origin who had married her, surrounded by a dark aura.

"Vivian, it's been a while."

"It has been a while, Evan."

"I figured it out—a way to send something, however small, to the past."

Evan spoke with ecstasy, but Vivian's face soured as she listened.

"So, you sacrificed all the people around you just for that? Even your family sent people, saying they would remove the family's disgrace themselves."

"Just for that? Ah, I see your pleasure center must be damaged from being penetrated all day by that Black bastard standing next to you. Well, I suppose there's no hope when you're addicted to sex. Blacks may not know much else, but they're impressive in one department. So, have you been shitting out that ten-month-old black turd well?"

Enraged, Vivian cast a spell to silence Evan. He easily dismantled the spell and looked at her with hatred.

"You promised to help! But you just went off to find ordinary happiness. Well, I didn't stop you either."

After saying this, his expression softened into a smile that made him look completely insane.

"Love must have been a good escape too."

The knights raised their swords and began approaching Evan. But he neither attacked nor avoided them.

Squelch.

The sound of metal cutting through flesh and sinking in echoed.

"E-Evan...?"

Vivian and the knights looked at Evan in shock. He was smiling brightly despite blood flowing from his mouth.

"My cheap emotions, my body, and escape are all I have..."

He fell to his knees. Everyone stared at him with bewildered eyes.

"Ah, Erica. I'm sorry. Hee."

He escaped again.

Through death.
